sections.paragraph:
ψευδαπόστολος, ου, ὁ one who claims to be an apostle without the divine commission necessary for the work, false/spurious/bogus apostle (cp. Polyaenus 5, 33, 6 ψευδάγγελοι=false messengers) 2 Cor 11:13. Just., D. 35, 3 has ψευδοαπόστολοι for ψευδοπροφῆται in citation of Mt 24:11, 24 (Mk 13:22).—S. lit. s.v. ψευδόμαρτυς. DELG s.v. ψεύδομαι B. TW.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1096
